45.reason: [The variable 'c' is used uninitialized in the first call to isalpha if fgetc returns EOF immediately; also, isalpha expects unsigned char or EOF, passing negative char values (on platforms where char is signed) may cause undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used uninitialized in the first call to isalpha if fgetc returns EOF immediately; also, isalpha expects unsigned char or EOF, passing negative char values (on platforms where char is signed) may cause undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used uninitialized in the first call to isalpha if fgetc returns EOF immediately; also, isalpha expects unsigned char or EOF, but passing potentially negative char values can cause undefined behavior. However, the primary issue is logic: it skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but the loop condition may not handle EOF properly before calling isalpha.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used before including <ctype.h>, which is required for isalpha(). This will cause a compilation error due to implicit function declaration or undefined reference.]
